executive_summary: The Iran-US armed conflict has reached a new phase of intensification with the confirmed killing of Iran's Supreme Leader Khamenei in a US-Israeli coordinated strike, triggering Iran's 12th wave of attacks against US and Israeli targets. Gulf states report intercepting hundreds of missiles/drones while their Patriot air defenses face depletion within 4 days. The Strait of Hormuz remains contested—CENTCOM denies closure but insurers have canceled coverage and IRGC threatens to fire on any transiting ship. US casualties have risen to 6 troops killed, with Iranian civilian deaths now exceeding 555.
whats_new: Qatar shot down two Iranian Su-24 jets and seven ballistic missiles—first reported Iranian air attack on a Gulf state
Iran struck Cyprus despite British neutrality, marking geographic expansion of Iranian retaliation
Missiles successfully evaded US air defense systems in Kuwait airspace—demonstrating advancing Iranian strike capabilities
Hezbollah launches limited to three rockets so far—lower proxy activity than expected on northern Israel front
US Secretary Rubio confirms US attacked Iran preemptively after learning Israel planned to strike, fearing Tehran would retaliate against US forces
confirmed_events: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei confirmed killed in Israeli strike on Tehran (Operation Epic Fury)
Over 1,000 IRGC members and 555 Iranian civilians confirmed dead in US-Israeli strikes—Israeli intelligence estimate
US forces destroyed all 11 Iranian warships in Gulf of Oman—footage published by CENTCOM
Gulf states (Bahrain, Qatar, UAE) intercepting hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones; Bahrain: 70 missiles/76 drones, UAE: 9 ballistic/6 cruise/148 drones
US and Israel struck Natanz nuclear facility—Iranian Atomic Energy Organization protested to IAEA
unverified_emerging: Claims of over 160 girls killed in US-Israeli attack on girls' school in Minab, southern Iran (Iraqji photo, unverified)
Norway-based Hengaw reports day-3 death toll at 1,500 including civilians and Iranian forces—cannot independently confirm
Investigators examining Iran war as possible motive in Austin, Texas shooting—potential homeland spillover link
Iranian missiles directly hit Muwaffaq Salti Air Base in Jordan causing power outages—visual confirmation exists but extent unclear

people_travel: Dubai flights disrupted—missile attacks shatter 'safe haven' perception for expats and business travelers
Air raid sirens active in Tel Aviv and northern Israel—civilian population under ongoing threat
Qatar's Al Udeid Air Base area reported explosions—US regional hub under threat
supply_chain: World's largest insurers have canceled war-risk coverage for vessels in Persian Gulf—commercial shipping effectively halted
Strait of Hormuz increasingly contested—20% of global oil supplies at risk despite CENTCOM stating it remains 'open'
IRGC threatens to 'set ablaze' any ship attempting to pass—explicit threat to global oil transit
market_macro: UN Secretary-General calls for de-escalation—diplomatic channel exists but no ceasefire progress
Trump signals US prepared for long war, won't rule out ground troops—conflict duration indefinite
Gulf states urgently requesting additional Patriot systems from US and Italy—defensive posture critical shortage

outlook_24_72h.base_case: The conflict will likely intensify over the next 24-72 hours. With Iran's Supreme Leader killed, Tehran will seek to demonstrate resolve through continued missile/drone salvos against US bases and Gulf partners. IRGC's Wave 12 operations confirm sustained offensive capacity. Gulf air defenses face critical depletion within 4 days without resupply. The Strait of Hormuz situation remains the primary wild card—if IRGC executes threats against commercial shipping, global oil supplies face severe disruption.
escalation_risks: IRGC executes threat to attack commercial vessels in Strait of Hormuz—potential global oil supply shock
Hezbollah significantly escalates attacks on northern Israel—opens multi-front war with Lebanon
Iranian cyberattacks on Gulf state infrastructure—water, power, financial systems
US ground troop deployment to Iran—Trump hasn't ruled this out
Russian/Chinese intervention to support Iran—geopolitical扩大
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: No clear de-escalation pathway exists in the near term. The killing of Iran's Supreme Leader removes a key figure for potential negotiations and hardens both sides' positions. UN Secretary-General's call for dialogue has gone unheeded. The only plausible paths would be: (1) a decisive US-Israeli military outcome that forces Tehran to negotiate under terms of weakness, or (2) mutual exhaustion after significant casualties on both sides. Neither appears imminent within the 24-72h window.
